title: Connor Wong’s 3-RBI night helped power Red Sox win, and it couldn’t come at a better time for him individually
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Connor Wong contributed three RBIs in the Red Sox's recent victory, boosting his standing amidst competition.
Wong, who lost his starting role, is vying for the backup catcher spot with his improved .245 batting average.
Interim manager Chad Tracy indicated Mickey Gasper as the primary catcher, focusing on match-ups against right-handed pitchers.

title: Sonny Gray continues to be great addition to Red Sox rotation, earns 6th win vs. Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray pitched six innings, allowing one run, and struck out seven in Red Sox's 9-1 win over the Guardians.
Gray's season ERA lowered to 3.06 after finishing May with a 2.00 ERA across five starts with Boston.
Red Sox are 7-3 in games started by Gray, who earned his sixth win of the season, amid Garrett Crochet's injury.

title: Here’s the full lineup of athletes and celebrities involved in Drake Maye’s charity softball game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Drake Maye's charity softball game is scheduled at Polar Park in Worcester on Sunday afternoon.
Coaches for the charity game are Mike Vrabel for Team Patriots and Josh McDaniels for Team Sox.
Notable participants include Julian Edelman, Zdeno Chara, Matt Rife, and Dave Portnoy among others.

title: Potential A.J. Brown Patriots-Eagles trade reportedly has ‘framework in place’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Patriots and Eagles reportedly have a trade framework in place for wide receiver A.J. Brown.
Philadelphia seeks a first-round pick; New England is reluctant, complicating final trade details.
The potential trade, expected post-June 1, would minimize Philadelphia's salary cap impact.

title: Did you hear it? Here’s what we know about a big boom heard around Mass.
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A loud boom heard around Greater Boston was likely caused by a bolide meteor reentry, according to meteorologists.
No earthquakes or explosions were reported, and officials confirmed no emergency incidents linked to the noise.
Hundreds of 911 calls were made across Eastern Massachusetts, but the noise posed no ongoing danger to the public.

title: Brayan Bello excels in bulk relief, but opener melts in Red Sox’ 4-3 loss to Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tyler Samaniego allowed four runs in the first inning, leading to the Red Sox's 4-3 loss to the Guardians.
Brayan Bello excelled in relief, allowing no runs and striking out five over seven innings, improving his relief ERA to 0.71.
Boston's loss drops them to 23-33, 10 games under .500, and keeps them last in the American League East.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo praises TD Garden environment amid trade rumors
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praises Boston Celtics fans for their passion during games, while trade rumors continue to swirl around him.
Boston Celtics and Miami Heat are closely linked to Antetokounmpo, though Miami is reportedly favored to acquire him.
Speculation involves Jaylen Brown as a potential trade piece for Antetokounmpo, with possible multi-team trade scenarios.

title: Harvard ‘Senior Sunrise’ cut short by cops
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Police dispersed Harvard's Senior Sunrise event on Weeks Footbridge due to crowd blocking and swimming attempts.
Massachusetts State Police, along with Cambridge and Harvard University police, responded to the early morning gathering.
The incident followed Harvard's commencement, where Conan O'Brien delivered the keynote address to the Class of 2026.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair' is now streaming on Peacock, featuring Uma Thurman in Quentin Tarantino's epic film saga.
'The Truman Show', starring Jim Carrey, is available on Paramount+ and YouTube, revisiting themes of surveillance and reality TV.
'The Four Seasons' Season 2 with Tina Fey and Steve Carell is now streaming on Netflix, continuing the comedy-drama series.

title: Red Sox predicted to draft 18-year-old third baseman in 2026 MLB Draft by ESPN
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: ESPN's Kiley McDaniel predicts Red Sox will draft Bo Lowrance, 18, as 20th pick in 2026 MLB Draft.
Scouts compare Bo Lowrance to three-time World Series champion Freddie Freeman; Lowrance is a left-handed third baseman.
Lowrance, from South Carolina, is committed to University of Virginia and may switch to first base in the future.
